Abstract

Nitrogen-cycling communities in the rhizosphere play a key role in plant nutrition.

Introduction

The rhizosphere is the narrow zone of soil directly influenced by root exudates. Root-adhering
soil was separated from roots by vortexing in phosphate buffer and centrifugation. We collected
root system samples from 5 replicate plots. The rhizosphere soil fraction was obtained by
washing roots thoroughly and collecting the wash. Metagenome libraries were prepared from
0.5 g of soil per sample.

Discussion
